description: Notification System Overhaul - Replace shell-out notifications with trait-based native HTTP supporting 8 backends

userStories.title: Define Notifier trait, Notification struct, and MultiNotifier
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Notifier trait and MultiNotifier dispatcher so that adding new notification backends is just implementing a trait.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/core/notify/ module directory with mod.rs, message.rs, error.rs
Notifier trait defined with name() -> &str and send(&Notification) -> Result<(), NotifyError>
Notification struct with subject: String, body: String, severity: Severity, fields: HashMap<String, String>
Severity enum with Success, Failure, Warning, Info variants
NotifyError type with service name (String) and message (String)
MultiNotifier struct with add(Box<dyn Notifier>) and send_all(&Notification) -> Vec<NotifyError>
All types are Send + Sync
Old src/core/notify.rs replaced by src/core/notify/mod.rs re-exporting the old resolve_notify_command for backward compat
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add ureq and lettre dependencies with feature flags
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ureq for native HTTP and lettre for SMTP so notifications don't depend on external binaries.
acceptanceCriteria: ureq added to Cargo.toml with json feature
lettre added to Cargo.toml, gated behind feature = 'email'
Feature flags defined: slack, discord, webhook, ntfy, telegram, email, msteams, gotify
Default features include: slack, discord, webhook, ntfy, telegram, email
No tokio or async runtime in dependency tree
cargo build succeeds with default features
cargo build --no-default-features succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SlackWebhook notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want Slack notifications via native HTTP so they work without curl installed.
acceptanceCriteria: SlackWebhook struct in src/core/notify/slack.rs implements Notifier trait
POST JSON payload to webhook URL via ureq
Message includes severity-colored attachment (green/red/orange/blue)
Workflow fields rendered in attachment fields
Gated behind feature = 'slack'
Unit test verifies payload JSON structure
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement GenericWebhook notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want generic webhook notifications via native HTTP for reliability.
acceptanceCriteria: GenericWebhook struct in src/core/notify/webhook.rs implements Notifier trait
POST JSON body with subject, body, severity, and fields
Gated behind feature = 'webhook'
Unit test verifies payload JSON structure
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement EmailNotifier with lettre
userStories.description: As a user, I want email notifications via SMTP so they don't depend on the system mail command.
acceptanceCriteria: EmailNotifier struct in src/core/notify/email.rs implements Notifier trait
Uses lettre crate for SMTP delivery
Supports TLS (STARTTLS and implicit)
SMTP credentials read from SMTP_USERNAME and SMTP_PASSWORD environment variables
Config parsed from email://user@host?smtp=smtp.example.com&port=587 URL scheme
Gated behind feature = 'email'
Unit test verifies message construction (no actual SMTP call)
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement DiscordWebhook notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send workflow notifications to Discord so my team gets alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: DiscordWebhook struct in src/core/notify/discord.rs implements Notifier trait
Sends Discord embed with severity-based color coding (0x2ECC71 green, 0xE74C3C red, 0xF39C12 orange, 0x3498DB blue)
Workflow fields rendered as inline embed fields
Gated behind feature = 'discord'
Unit test verifies embed JSON structure
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement TelegramBot notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send workflow notifications to Telegram so I get mobile alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: TelegramBot struct in src/core/notify/telegram.rs implements Notifier trait
Uses Bot API sendMessage endpoint with MarkdownV2 formatting
Severity icons in message prefix (checkmark, X, warning, info)
Proper MarkdownV2 character escaping for special chars
Bot token and chat_id parsed from telegram://BOT_TOKEN@CHAT_ID URL
Gated behind feature = 'telegram'
Unit test verifies message formatting and escaping
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement ntfy notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send notifications via ntfy for self-hosted push alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Ntfy struct in src/core/notify/ntfy.rs implements Notifier trait
POST message body to topic URL with headers for title, priority, tags
Severity mapped to ntfy priority levels (5=urgent for failure, 4=high for warning, 3=default for success/info)
Optional auth token support via token query parameter
Config parsed from ntfy://ntfy.sh/my-topic or ntfy://my-server.local/topic?token=tk_xxx
Gated behind feature = 'ntfy'
Unit test verifies header construction
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement TeamsWebhook notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send workflow notifications to Microsoft Teams.
acceptanceCriteria: TeamsWebhook struct in src/core/notify/msteams.rs implements Notifier trait
Uses Adaptive Card JSON format for the new Workflows/Power Automate connector
Severity-colored card header
Workflow fields rendered as Adaptive Card facts
Gated behind feature = 'msteams'
Unit test verifies Adaptive Card payload structure
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement Gotify notifier
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send notifications via Gotify for self-hosted push notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Gotify struct in src/core/notify/gotify.rs implements Notifier trait
POST to /message endpoint with title, message, priority fields
Severity mapped to Gotify priority levels (8=high for failure, 5=normal for warning, 2=low for success/info)
App token parsed from gotify://my-server.local?token=APP_TOKEN URL
Gated behind feature = 'gotify'
Unit test verifies payload construction
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement URL-scheme resolver for all backends
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a unified URL-scheme resolver so config strings are parsed into the correct Notifier trait objects.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve.rs parses all 8 URL schemes (slack://, discord://, telegram://, teams://, ntfy://, gotify://, webhook://, email://) into Box<dyn Notifier>
Unknown schemes return a descriptive NotifyError
Environment variable references ($VAR) expanded in URLs before parsing
Feature-gated: unavailable schemes return a compile-time or descriptive error
Unit tests for each scheme's parsing
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Refactor executor to use trait-based notifications
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the executor to use the new trait-based system so the old shell-out code is fully removed.
acceptanceCriteria: build_notification() function creates Notification from RunLog + template vars
build_notifiers_from_config() uses resolve.rs to construct Vec<Box<dyn Notifier>> from config
Old run_notify() shell-out function removed from executor.rs
Old NotifyCommand enum and resolve_notify_command() removed (replaced by new resolver)
Notification errors logged but never block workflow completion
ExecutionEvent variants updated if needed for TUI display
All existing integration tests pass
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Support multi-target notification config
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send notifications to multiple targets so failures go to both Slack and my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: on_failure and on_success accept both a single string and an array of strings in YAML and TOML
Serde deserialize_with string_or_vec for backward compatibility
Single string deserialized as one-element vec
All targets notified independently (one failure does not skip others)
Works in both config.toml (global) and workflow YAML (per-workflow)
Unit test for single-string and array deserialization
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add severity-based notification routing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to route different severity levels to different targets.
acceptanceCriteria: New channels config format with target (string) and on (array of severities) fields
on accepts: success, failure, warning, info
Old on_failure/on_success format still works alongside channels
Per-workflow notify.channels overrides or extends global config
Unit test for routing logic (correct targets selected per severity)
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Implement global + per-workflow notify merge
userStories.description: As a user, I want per-workflow notifications to add to global defaults rather than replace them.
acceptanceCriteria: By default workflow-level notify merges with global config.toml notify targets
Workflow can set notify_override: true to fully replace global targets
Merge deduplicates identical target URLs
Unit test for merge and override behavior
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add retry with exponential backoff
userStories.description: As a user, I want failed notifications to retry automatically so transient network issues don't cause missed alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: RetryConfig struct with max_attempts (default 3), initial_delay_ms (default 1000), backoff_factor (default 2.0)
Configurable globally in config.toml under [notify.retry]
Retries are per-notifier (one failing service does not delay others)
Final failure logged with all attempt error messages
Retry uses std::thread::sleep (no async)
Unit test for retry logic with a mock notifier that fails then succeeds
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add per-service rate limiting
userStories.description: As a developer, I want per-service rate limiting so we don't hit API limits when workflows run frequently.
acceptanceCriteria: RateLimiter tracks sends per service name per time window using Instant-based sliding window
Default limits: Discord 30/min, Telegram 30/sec, others 60/min
Excess notifications dropped with a warning log message
Configurable per-service in config.toml under [notify.rate_limit]
In-process only (no persistence, resets on restart)
Unit test for rate limiting behavior
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add rich service-specific formatting
userStories.description: As a user, I want notifications to use each service's native rich formatting for readable alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack: Block Kit with header, section blocks, and colored sidebar
Discord: Embeds with title, description, colored sidebar, and inline fields
Telegram: MarkdownV2 with bold subject, inline code for step output, severity icon
Teams: Adaptive Cards with TextBlock header, FactSet for fields, colored accent
Fallback to plain text body if rich formatting construction fails
Existing unit tests updated to verify rich payload structure
Typecheck passes
cargo test passes
